272:      search:   "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/search?query={query}&format=json&pageSize={limit}"
273:      fulltext: "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/{source}/{id}/fullTextXML"
275:  - adapter_id: pmc
279:    query_dialects: [pmc_id]
280:    identifier_types: [pmcid, pmid]
284:      PubMed Central OA full-text article pages by PMCID. A retrieval TARGET — its landing pages are a
287:      fulltext: "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/{pmcid}/"
368:    # (the field-agnostic indices openalex/doaj/core/openaire/europe_pmc cover discover.index). This is
=== ls routes_* ===
ls: cannot access 'scripts/routes_*': No such file or directory
=== yaml head ===
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SOURCE ROUTES — the DECLARATIVE, CAPABILITY-KEYED discovery table (Sol plan 4, item 2).
#
# THE STRATEGY THIS ENCODES
#   The old strategy — "choose the canonical (usually paywalled) work, then chase a free copy of it" —
#   had a 19% hit rate, because it started from the version we were LEAST likely to be able to read.
#   This table encodes the inverse: OA-FIRST DISCOVERY, then VERSION PURSUIT. We enumerate what is
#   openly readable FIRST (DOAJ / PMC / Europe PMC / OpenAlex / CORE / OpenAIRE), we resolve identity
#   and the full set of locations (Crossref / OpenAlex / Unpaywall — and we NEVER treat their version
#   label as final), and only then do we pursue the specific citable expression the plan's policy
#   requires (e.g. the journal Version of Record for a journal-only question).
#
# WHY THIS IS DATA AND NOT CODE
#   The router MUST NOT "know" that economists post to NBER or that clinicians register trials at
#   ClinicalTrials.gov. That knowledge is a hardcoded topic gate — the exact tell this project keeps
#   failing on. So it lives HERE, as ONE ROW PER ROUTE, keyed by the EVIDENCE CAPABILITY the route
#   supplies. Adding a domain that these adapters already serve (a new jurisdiction's courts, a new
#   preprint server) is a NEW ROW in this file and ZERO lines of Python. scripts/source_router.py is a
#   generic matcher + set-cover engine over this table; it contains no adapter name and no topic word.
#
# HOW A QUESTION REACHES A ROUTE  (all three stages are data-driven; see source_router.py)
#   1. compile_contract(question)                       -> a research contract (research_contract.py)
#   2. role_requirements  (this file)  x  contract      -> the REQUIRED evidence roles for this plan
#   3. SET COVER  required_roles  over  routes[*].evidence_roles -> the routes that FIRE
#   The admissibility of what a route returns is a SEPARATE axis (source_policy over document_types):
#   a preprint route may FIRE for discovery/version-pursuit yet yield nothing CITABLE under a
#   journal-only policy. Firing and citability are never the same decision.
#
# EVERY LIVE ATTEMPT GOES THROUGH acquisition.Acquirer, so it lands on the event ledger and the
# distinct outcomes stay distinct:
#   FETCHED | NOT_FOUND | ACCESS_DENIED | THROTTLED | TRANSIENT_ERROR | LANDING_PAGE | ABSTRACT_ONLY |
#   WRONG_WORK | CORRUPT_EXTRACTION.
# Only NOT_FOUND, across every APPLICABLE route that genuinely answered, supports "we did not locate an
# accessible copy." A 429 (THROTTLED) never does, and neither does a 403 (ACCESS_DENIED).
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

schema_version: 1
registry_version: sr-1
mailto_env: POLARIS_MAILTO          # the polite-pool contact the router threads into query dialects
fetch_date: "2026-07-14"

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# THE ROLE VOCABULARY — an EVIDENCE CAPABILITY, not a domain. A route SUPPLIES roles; a plan REQUIRES
# roles; set cover joins them. The four `discover.*/resolve.*/fetch.*` roles are the OA-first backbone
# every scholarly plan needs. The `evidence.*` roles are field-scoped and fire only when the plan's own
# vocabulary calls for them. The dotted `capability.field` form is deliberate: the capability is the
# noun, the field is what a new domain edits.
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
roles:
  # -- OA-first backbone (always required for a scholarly artifact) -----------------------------------
  discover.index:        "Enumerate candidate scholarly works by topic (a bibliographic index)."
  resolve.identity:      "Resolve a work's identity, dates and version RELATIONS from an identifier. NOT full text."
  resolve.locations:     "Enumerate the OA copy LOCATIONS/versions of a known work. The version label is a hint, never final."
  fetch.oa_fulltext:     "Retrieve open-access FULL TEXT (XML/PDF/HTML) that we can actually read and mine."
  # -- field-scoped evidence kinds (fire on the plan's own evidence vocabulary) -----------------------
  evidence.peer_reviewed:          "Peer-reviewed journal articles — the citable class under a journal-only / peer-reviewed policy."
  evidence.working_paper.economics: "Economics/policy working papers as SEPARATE works (peer review changes the numbers)."
  evidence.preprint.general:       "General/CS/physics/math preprints as separate works."
  evidence.preprint.biomedical:    "Biomedical/health preprints as separate works."
  evidence.working_paper.social_legal: "Social-science and legal scholarship / working papers."
  evidence.trial_registration:     "Prospective clinical-trial registrations and results."
  evidence.clinical_guideline:     "Clinical practice guidelines and evidence syntheses from guideline bodies."
  evidence.case_law:               "Primary case law — judicial opinions and their holdings."
  evidence.statute_regulation:     "Primary statutes, regulations and official legal instruments."
  evidence.regulatory_guidance:    "Regulator guidance, decisions and official filings."
  evidence.standard:               "Technical standards and normative specifications."
  evidence.dataset:                "Research datasets and their DOIs."

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ROLE REQUIREMENTS — how the CONTRACT decides which roles the plan needs. Each rule matches DATA
# tokens against the plan's ROUTING TEXT (question + review_subject + method_designs + unit_levels +
# core_concept aliases + framing devices + genre + geographies — assembled by source_router.py). The
# tokens are FIELD-NATIVE vocabulary a real contract in that field prints; the matcher is generic and
# lives in code, the vocabulary lives here. `default: true` = always required (the OA-first backbone).
# `any_of` = required if the routing text contains ANY listed token as a whole word/phrase.
#
# OVER-FIRING IS SAFE (we look in one more place); UNDER-FIRING is the danger. So triggers are
# INCLUSIVE. The one hard invariant the demo checks: a clinical plan must NOT acquire the economics
# working-paper role — so `evidence.working_paper.economics` is triggered ONLY by economics-native
# terms, none of which a clinical or legal question carries.

input.command: cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel && sed -n '190,310p' config/source_routes.yaml

content.content:
#   identifier_types     identifiers it accepts/resolves.
#   version_resolvers    the version-pursuit mechanism it OFFERS (empty = it is a target, not a resolver).
#   rate_policy          politeness for acquisition._space (host + min spacing + key/pool).
#   coverage_note        what it is and is not.
#   endpoints            real URL templates for the live probes (may be empty where there is no clean API;
#                        `discovery_via` then names the resolver route that finds this route's works).
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
routes:

  # ============================ OA-FIRST BACKBONE: index / identity / locations / fulltext ===========

  - adapter_id: openalex
    evidence_roles: [discover.index, resolve.identity, resolve.locations, evidence.peer_reviewed]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript, preprint, working_paper]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [openalex_search, openalex_filter]
    identifier_types: [doi, openalex_id, pmid, pmcid, mag]
    version_resolvers: [openalex_locations]   # primary_location + locations[] across host venues
    rate_policy: {host: api.openalex.org, min_spacing_s: 1.0, needs_key: false, pool: mailto}
    coverage_note: >-
      250M+ works with per-work location enumeration and version relations. Its `host_venue`/`version`
      labels are HINTS to pursue, never a final verdict on which expression we hold.
    endpoints:
      search:   "https://api.openalex.org/works?search={query}&per_page={limit}&mailto={mailto}"
      identity: "https://api.openalex.org/works/doi:{doi}?mailto={mailto}"

  - adapter_id: crossref
    evidence_roles: [resolve.identity]
    document_types: [journal_version, proceedings_version, working_paper, preprint]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [crossref_query]
    identifier_types: [doi]
    version_resolvers: [crossref_relations, crossref_dates]   # is-preprint-of / is-version-of, published dates
    rate_policy: {host: api.crossref.org, min_spacing_s: 1.0, needs_key: false, pool: mailto}
    coverage_note: >-
      IDENTITY, DATES AND RELATIONS ONLY — never treated as a full-text source. Its relation edges
      (is-preprint-of, is-version-of) are the backbone of version pursuit from a preprint to the VoR.
    endpoints:
      search:   "https://api.crossref.org/works?query={query}&rows={limit}&mailto={mailto}"
      identity: "https://api.crossref.org/works/{doi}?mailto={mailto}"

  - adapter_id: unpaywall
    evidence_roles: [resolve.locations]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [unpaywall_doi]
    identifier_types: [doi]
    version_resolvers: [unpaywall_oa_locations]   # best_oa_location + oa_locations[] with host_type + version
    rate_policy: {host: api.unpaywall.org, min_spacing_s: 1.0, needs_key: false, pool: email}
    coverage_note: >-
      Enumerates OA locations for a DOI (repository vs publisher, submitted/accepted/published). We take
      its `version` as a claim to VERIFY against the fetched bytes (event_ledger.derive_semantic_binding),
      not as the answer.
    endpoints:
      identity: "https://api.unpaywall.org/v2/{doi}?email={mailto}"

  - adapter_id: doaj
    evidence_roles: [discover.index, evidence.peer_reviewed]
    document_types: [journal_version]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [doaj_query]
    identifier_types: [doi, issn]
    version_resolvers: []
    rate_policy: {host: doaj.org, min_spacing_s: 1.2, needs_key: false, pool: public}
    coverage_note: >-
      Directory of Open Access Journals — 13M+ articles from vetted OA journals. Its entire index is
      peer-reviewed journal expressions, so it is the strongest single index for a journal-only policy.
    endpoints:
      search: "https://doaj.org/api/v2/search/articles/{query}?pageSize={limit}"

  - adapter_id: europe_pmc
    evidence_roles: [discover.index, fetch.oa_fulltext]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript, preprint]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [europepmc_query]
    identifier_types: [doi, pmid, pmcid]
    version_resolvers: [europepmc_fulltext]
    rate_policy: {host: www.ebi.ac.uk, min_spacing_s: 1.0, needs_key: false, pool: public}
    coverage_note: >-
      Life-sciences + cross-discipline index over PMC with OA full-text XML retrieval. Full text where
      the licence permits; abstract-only otherwise (which the reducer reports as ABSTRACT_ONLY, not a gap).
    endpoints:
      search:   "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/search?query={query}&format=json&pageSize={limit}"
      fulltext: "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/{source}/{id}/fullTextXML"

  - adapter_id: pmc
    evidence_roles: [fetch.oa_fulltext]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [pmc_id]
    identifier_types: [pmcid, pmid]
    version_resolvers: []
    rate_policy: {host: www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov, min_spacing_s: 1.2, needs_key: false, pool: public}
    coverage_note: >-
      PubMed Central OA full-text article pages by PMCID. A retrieval TARGET — its landing pages are a
      known LANDING_PAGE trap, which is exactly why completeness is derived from the bytes, not the URL.
    endpoints:
      fulltext: "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/{pmcid}/"

  - adapter_id: core
    evidence_roles: [discover.index, fetch.oa_fulltext, resolve.locations]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript, preprint, working_paper]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [core_v3_query]
    identifier_types: [doi, core_id]
    version_resolvers: [core_fulltext_links]
    rate_policy: {host: api.core.ac.uk, min_spacing_s: 2.0, needs_key: true, key_env: CORE_API_KEY, pool: keyed}
    coverage_note: >-
      Aggregates 200M+ full texts from institutional & subject REPOSITORIES — the channel most likely to
      hold a readable copy when the publisher blocks us. Needs a free API key (rate_policy.needs_key).
    endpoints:
      search: "https://api.core.ac.uk/v3/search/works?q={query}&limit={limit}"

  - adapter_id: openaire
    evidence_roles: [discover.index, resolve.locations, evidence.dataset]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript, preprint, working_paper, dataset]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [openaire_query]
    identifier_types: [doi, openaire_id, handle]
    version_resolvers: [openaire_instances]
    rate_policy: {host: api.openaire.eu, min_spacing_s: 1.5, needs_key: false, pool: public}

content.content:
#: Terminal conclusions. A component may not state these — the reducer derives them.
#:
#: Sol V9 §1, verbatim: "The adapter must not write FULLTEXT, THIS_WORK, VERSION_OF_RECORD, ADMISSIBLE,
#: or an expression edge. Those are reducer outputs derived from immutable metadata and document bytes."
#: FULLTEXT, ADMISSIBLE and `same work` were already here. THIS_WORK and VERSION_OF_RECORD were not —
#: and an adapter that may write `version_of_record` into a payload has been handed, by the back door,
#: precisely the authority the V9 P0 was about taking away from it. A repository saying `acceptedVersion`
#: is an OBSERVATION; a component writing `version_of_record` is a VERDICT.
RESERVED_VALUES = (
    'fulltext', 'full text', 'full-text',
    'complete', 'route_complete',
    'no evidence', 'no free copy', 'no free text', 'not available', 'nothing found',
    'same work', 'same_work', 'this work', 'this_work', 'different work', 'different_work',
    'version equivalent', 'version of record', 'version_of_record',
    'high quality', 'high_quality', 'low quality', 'low_quality',
    'paywalled', 'still paywalled',
    'supported', 'searched_none', 'conflicted',
    'admissible', 'inadmissible', 'eligible',
    'verified', 'span-verified', 'fabrication-proof',
)

#: Keys that ARE labels. Naming one is an attempt to set the label directly.
RESERVED_KEYS = (
    'content_status', 'status', 'label', 'verdict', 'quality', 'tier', 'grade',
    'fulltext_source', 'is_fulltext', 'route_complete', 'complete', 'coverage',
    'coverage_status', 'eligibility', 'binding', 'admissible', 'evidence_status',
)

#: Fields that are RAW TEXT QUOTED FROM THE WORLD. A PDF may contain any word at all; that is not
#: the component asserting it. These are exempt from the value scan (never from the key scan).
#: NOTE what is NOT here: `note`. A free-text `note` is the COMPONENT talking, not the world, and it
#: is precisely where a conclusion would be smuggled in ("note: no free copy exists"). It stays
#: scanned. (The guard caught exactly that in this file's own replay code, and the fix was to reword
#: the note into an observation — not to exempt the field.)
#: `locator` is here for the same reason `url` is: IT IS A URL. Publishers put our own reserved words
#: inside their paths (`/articles/PMC.../?report=fulltext`, `/science/article/.../fulltext`), and a
#: guard that refused to record THE ADDRESS THE BYTES CAME FROM — because the address contains the
#: string "fulltext" — would have made the locator unrecordable and left us exactly where we were:
#: holding an NBER working paper under a URL pointing at aeaweb.org.
#: `requested_venue` sits beside `requested_title` for the same reason: it is a NAME, quoted from the
#: bibliography. There is a journal called `Information & Management` and there could be one called
#: `Complete Systems`; a guard that refused to record which journal we asked about, because the
#: journal's name contains one of our reserved words, would be a guard against nothing.
VERBATIM_KEYS = frozenset({
    'header_text', 'identity_window', 'span', 'document_title', 'byline', 'snippet', 'raw', 'title',
    'reason_text', 'requested_title', 'requested_venue', 'url', 'locator', 'doi', 'venue',
})


def _scan_value(k: str, v, bad: list[str], path: str = '') -> None:
    """Scan ONE value, RECURSIVELY.

    IT DID NOT RECURSE. The scan ran over `payload.items()` and tested `isinstance(v, str)` — so a
    conclusion one level down was invisible to it:

        emit(..., adapter_observations={'content_status': 'FULLTEXT'})    # ACCEPTED. Silently.

    A guard that only inspects the top level of a nested structure is a guard you step over by adding
    a dict, and "the checks certify a lane the fabrication no longer uses" is the sentence this whole
    project is trying to stop writing. Payloads stay flat by convention; this makes it true by force.
    """
    where = f'{path}{k}'
    if isinstance(v, str):
        vl = v.lower().strip()
        for r in RESERVED_VALUES:
            # match the whole value or a word-bounded occurrence — not a substring of a word
            if vl == r or re.search(rf'(?<![a-z0-9_]){re.escape(r)}(?![a-z0-9_])', vl):
                bad.append(f'{where}={v!r} states the conclusion {r!r}; emit the OBSERVATION instead '
                           f'(word_count / http_status / byline), and let the reducer conclude')
                return
    elif isinstance(v, dict):
        for k2, v2 in v.items():
            if str(k2).lower() in RESERVED_KEYS:
                bad.append(f'key {where}.{k2!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may '
                           f'not set it, and burying it one level down does not make it an observation')
                continue
            if str(k2).lower() in VERBATIM_KEYS:
                continue
            _scan_value(str(k2), v2, bad, path=f'{where}.')
    elif isinstance(v, (list, tuple)):
        for i, v2 in enumerate(v):
            _scan_value(f'[{i}]', v2, bad, path=where)
=== observe_text ===
def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.

    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
    """
    words = text.split()
    header = text[:header_chars]
    # THE ARTICLE'S OWN FRONT MATTER, with any repository cover sheet segmented off it. The DOIs found
    # HERE are the document's own claim about which article it is — as distinct from the DOI a library
    # printed on a cover page, which is the library's claim about what it filed.
    cover, article_front = segment_front_matter(text, header_chars)
    front_dois = sorted({d.rstrip('.').lower() for d in _DOI_RE.findall(article_front)})
    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
    return {
        'word_count': len(words),
        'char_count': len(text),
        'readable_word_count': real_words,
        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(header)),
        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(header)),
        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
        'header_real_words': header_real,
        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
        'identity_window': header,
        'header_text': header[:400],
        # ---- SOL V9 §5: IDENTITY FROM THE FETCHED CONTENT ITSELF -------------------------------
        # The DOI the ARTICLE prints on itself (JATS article-DOI, PDF front matter, HTML header) — with
        # any repository COVER SHEET segmented off, because a cover sheet's DOI is the library's opinion
        # of what it deposited, not the document's testimony about what it is.
        'front_matter_dois': front_dois,
        'cover_sheet_chars': len(cover),
        'cover_sheet_text': cover[:400],
        'article_front_matter': article_front[:400],
        # A BYLINE IS PRESENT — or it is not. `False` means the reducer may not reason about
        # disjointness AT ALL: an absent byline is not a disjoint one. (A cookie banner names no author
        # because it is not a document, not because a stranger wrote it.)
        'byline_present': bool(_BYLINE_CUE.search(article_front) or _BYLINE.search(article_front)),
    }


# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# REDUCERS — the ONLY place a label may come into existence.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ---- 1. backend outcome ----------------------------------------------------------------------
=== derive_content_profile ===
def derive_content_profile(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[str, dict]:
    """FULLTEXT IS EARNED — AGAINST THE PROFILE FOR ITS KIND. It is never a thing a fetcher declares,
    and it is never a number this module keeps to itself."""
    best = _best_holding(events)
    if best is None:
        return C_CITATION, {'reason': 'no content was ever profiled'}
    _manif, _prof, cls, info = best
    n = len(_holdings(events))
    if n > 1:
        info = {**info, 'n_documents_held': n,
                'reason': f'{info["reason"]} (the best of {n} documents held for this work)'}
    return cls, info


# ---- 4. semantic binding ---------------------------------------------------------------------

SAME_WORK        = 'SAME_WORK'              # this IS the journal article
VERSION_PUBLISHED = 'VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED'  # a repository copy OF the published version
VERSION_PREPRINT = 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'    # a working paper. A DIFFERENT SOURCE until proven equal.
DIFFERENT_WORK   = 'DIFFERENT_WORK'         # we fetched somebody else's paper
UNRESOLVED       = 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'


def _norm(s: str) -> set[str]:
    stop = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'of', 'and', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'from', 'with'}
    return {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]+', (s or '').lower()) if w not in stop and len(w) > 2}

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""CI CANARY — FAILS IF THE FAITHFULNESS GATE IS BYPASSED.

WHY THIS EXISTS, IN ONE PARAGRAPH:
I built `synthesis_contract.py`, ran 14 adversarial attacks I had written myself, watched it print "ZERO
FALSE ADMISSIONS", and reported it as working. An adversarial reviewer then found that `validate()` was
imported at `cellcog_composer.py:49` and **never called anywhere in the repo except its own self_test()**.
The gate was a closed loop: invoked only by its own test, fed its own examples, printing green.

WHY IT WAS REWRITTEN — AND THIS IS THE WHOLE LESSON OF THE NIGHT:
This canary then went 16/16 GREEN while SIX ADVERSARY ATTACKS SUCCEEDED and nothing had been weakened.
It was not defeated. It was ORPHANED. It drove `cellcog_composer._clean()` with hand-built dicts —
`{'authors': [...], 'span': '...'}` — and the fabrication had moved to a lane where cards carry
manifestations and content hashes. **THE CHECKS CERTIFIED A LANE THE FABRICATION NO LONGER USED.**

A canary that tests a dead function is worse than no canary, because it is green.

So every fixture here is now a BOUND card in a real graph (`_test_fixtures.py`), and every check drives
THE CODE THAT ACTUALLY SHIPS: `report_ast.validate_report()` -> `publisher.publish()` -> the sealed
release directory. If the composer, the AST or the publisher stops calling the gate, this goes red.

    python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import ast
import inspect
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))

import provenance as P                                                    # noqa: E402
import report_ast as A                                                    # noqa: E402
import publisher                                                          # noqa: E402
import _test_fixtures                                                     # noqa: E402
from report_ast import Attributed, Clause, Owned, CardBundle              # noqa: E402

COMPOSER = ROOT / 'scripts' / 'cellcog_composer.py'
MINER = ROOT / 'scripts' / 'evidence_miner.py'
BOUND = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards_bound.json'
RELEASE = publisher.RELEASE / 'report.md'

fails: list[str] = []


def check(name: str, ok: bool, detail: str = '') -> None:
    print(f"  [{'PASS' if ok else '**FAIL**'}] {name}")
    if detail and not ok:
        print(f'            {detail}')
    if not ok:
        fails.append(name)


print('=== CI CANARY: IS THE FAITHFULNESS GATE ACTUALLY ON THE CRITICAL PATH? ===\n')

content.content:
the body we fetched does NOT contain that abstract's distinctive vocabulary;
        therefore the body is not a rendering of this work.
    """
    head = text[:20000]
    authors = [a for a in (work.authors or []) if len(a) > 2]
    named = [a for a in authors if re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(a) + r'\b', head, re.I)]
    twords = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (work.title or '').lower())}
    tfound = {w for w in twords if w in head.lower()}
    # A title of one or two distinctive words is not a test: "Rise of the Machines" yields {machines},
    # which the maths paper contains. Below three, the title signal is UNUSABLE, not passing.
    title_usable = len(twords) >= 3

    overlap: float | None = None
    vocab = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (independent_abstract or '').lower())
             if w not in _FUNCTION_WORDS}
    if (len((independent_abstract or '').split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_ABSTRACT_WORDS
            and len(vocab) >= _IDENT_MIN_VOCAB
            and len(text.split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_BODY_WORDS):
        low = text.lower()
        overlap = round(sum(1 for w in vocab if w in low) / len(vocab), 3)

    if not readable:
        # You cannot judge identity from bytes you cannot read. Autor (2003)'s authors and title are
        # absent because they are (cid:11)(cid:3)... -- that is a fact about the FONT, not the paper.
        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'bytes are not readable — identity cannot be judged'
    elif overlap is not None and overlap < CONTRADICTED_VOCAB_OVERLAP:
        verdict = 'CONTRADICTED'
        basis = (f'the body contains only {overlap:.0%} of the distinctive vocabulary of this work’s '
                 f'own abstract (genuine bodies on this corpus: 97.5–100%) — THESE ARE NOT ITS BYTES')
    elif named:
        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(named)}/{len(authors)} claimed authors named in the text'
    elif overlap is not None and overlap >= 0.90:
        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'body contains {overlap:.0%} of its own abstract’s vocabulary'
    elif title_usable and len(tfound) >= 0.8 * len(twords):
        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(tfound)}/{len(twords)} distinctive title words present'
    else:
        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'no positive evidence either way (absence proves nothing)'

    return dict(verdict=verdict, basis=basis,
                authors_named=len(named), authors_claimed=len(authors),
                title_words_found=len(tfound), title_words=len(twords), title_usable=title_usable,
                abstract_vocab_overlap=overlap)


def derive_expression_kind(text: str, work_kind: str = 'study') -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Which VERSION do these bytes say they are? Derived from self-identifying furniture.

    Returns ('unknown', ...) freely. `unknown` is the honest answer for a PDF body with its title
    page mangled, and an honest `unknown` costs us evidence -- which is the correct price.

    THE SCHOLARLY VERSION TAXONOMY APPLIES ONLY TO SCHOLARLY WORKS. There is no preprint of a statute
    and no accepted manuscript of a judicial opinion; the authoritative text IS the artifact, and the
    registry says so. Running the NBER/arXiv/"accepted manuscript" regexes over a court opinion does
    not find nothing — it finds whatever the opinion happens to QUOTE, which is how a whole-text scan
    for "NBER Working Paper" once read the bibliography and called the JEP article a working paper.
    """
    fam = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind or 'study')
    if fam is not None:
        ex = KIND_PROFILE[fam]['expression']
        return ex, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — the version taxonomy of a {fam.replace("_", " ")} is '
                    f'not the scholarly one; these bytes are its {ex.replace("_", " ")}')
    head = text[:12000]
    wp, pre, am, jr = (_WP_MARK.search(head), _PREPRINT_MARK.search(head),
                       _AM_MARK.search(head), _JOURNAL_MARK.search(head))
    # Order matters: an accepted manuscript deposited in a repository carries the journal's citation
    # block too, so the AM marker must be read BEFORE the journal marker or every AM reads as typeset.
    if am:
        return 'accepted_manuscript', f'bytes say: {am.group(0)[:48]!r}'
    if wp:
        return 'working_paper', f'bytes say: {wp.group(0)[:48]!r}'
    if pre:
        return 'preprint', f'bytes say: {pre.group(0)[:48]!r}'
    if jr:
        return 'journal_version', f'typeset journal furniture: {jr.group(0)[:48]!r}'
    return 'unknown', 'no self-identifying version furniture in the first 12,000 chars'


def profile(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
            fetch_outcome: str = 'ok') -> dict:
    """THE CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT PROFILE. Replaces MIN_WORDS entirely.

    Every field is derived FROM THE BYTES. `complete` is judged against the profile FOR THIS KIND,
    so a short judicial opinion is complete and a 535-word cookie banner is not.
    """
    text = text or ''
    words = len(text.split())
    ex = extractability(text)
    ident = identity_agreement(text, work, independent_abstract, readable=ex['verdict'] == 'CLEAN')

    view = View(text)
    secs = sections_of(view)
    present = sorted({name for _, _, name in secs if name not in ('front', 'body')})
    result_secs = [s for s in present if s in RESULT_BEARING]   # REPORTED. GATES NOTHING. See above.
    # body/chrome ratio, measured on the bytes -- not on a word count.
    chrome_hits = len(_CHROME.findall(text))
    body_chars = sum(e - s for s, e, name in secs if SECTION_WEIGHT.get(name, 0.5) > 0)
    body_ratio = round(body_chars / max(1, len(text)), 3)
    chrome_per_kw = round(1000 * chrome_hits / max(1, words), 2)

    work_kind = work.kind or 'study'
    family = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind)      # None => scholarly; else the kind is FIXED by the work
=== stamps ===
380:_CHROME = re.compile(
381-    r'website uses cookies|accept\s+(?:all\s+)?cookies|by clicking the "accept" button|'
382-    r'enable javascript|sign in to (?:your account|continue)|create an account|'
383-    r'this site requires|subscribe to (?:read|view)|purchase access|add to cart|'
384-    # ...the repository LANDING PAGES that fooled us: Oxford ORA, PubMed Central, generic portals.
385-    r'skip to main content|we welcome feedback|send message|research archive|'
386-    r'privacy policy|terms of use|all rights reserved|official websites use',
387-    re.I)
388:_PUBLISHED_STAMP = re.compile(r'published version|version of record|\(refereed\)|original citation', re.I)
389-#: PREPRINT / WORKING-PAPER STAMPS — a DATA-DRIVEN REGISTRY, not a wall of ifs. Each row is
390-#: (repository/series key, header-regex fragment). ADDING A NEW REPOSITORY IS A ONE-LINE DATA EDIT.
391-#:
392-#: A stamp in a document's OWN header is evidence the manifestation is a WORKING-PAPER / PREPRINT
393-#: EXPRESSION — NOT the journal version of record. Peer review CHANGES NUMBERS (Acemoglu & Restrepo's
394-#: robot effect: 0.37pp in the NBER working paper, 0.2pp in the published JPE), so under a journal-only
395-#: policy a stamped manifestation is a DISCOVERY LEAD, never journal evidence (derive_semantic_binding
396-#: -> VERSION_PREPRINT -> derive_eligibility -> DISCOVERY_LEAD).
--
427:_PREPRINT_STAMP = re.compile(
428-    '|'.join(f'(?P<{key}>{pat})' for key, pat in PREPRINT_STAMP_REGISTRY), re.I)
429-
430-
431-def preprint_stamp_key(text: str) -> str | None:
432-    """The registry key of the FIRST preprint/working-paper stamp in `text`, or None.
433-
434-    A pure OBSERVATION helper — it CONCLUDES NOTHING. derive_semantic_binding() decides what a stamp
435-    MEANS (a DIFFERENT SOURCE until version equivalence is proven); this only reports which one is
--
462:_DOI_RE = re.compile(r'\b10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s"\'<>,;)\]]+', re.I)
463-
464-#: IS THERE A BYLINE AT ALL? Sol V9 §5 lets DIFFERENT_WORK be reached by "an incompatible foreign title
465-#: PLUS A DISJOINT BYLINE" — and BOTH halves are load-bearing. A byline that is ABSENT is not a byline
466-#: that is disjoint, and the difference is the whole of this project's disease.
467-#:
468-#: This was not academic. My first cut of the rule read "no requested author appears in the front matter"
469-#: as disjointness, and the router's own demo convicted it INSIDE A MINUTE: the cookie-banner fixture —
470-#: "This website uses cookies. Sign in to continue." — came out DIFFERENT_WORK. A landing page names no
--
480:_BYLINE = re.compile(r'[A-Z][a-z]+\s+[A-Z][\w.-]+\s*(?:,|\band\b)\s*[A-Z][a-z]+\s+[A-Z][\w.-]+')
481-
482-
483:def segment_front_matter(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> tuple[str, str]:
484-    """-> (cover_sheet_text, article_front_matter). Sol V9 §5: "Repository cover sheets are SEGMENTED
485-    from article front matter."
486-
487-    The boundary is taken at the first form-feed after the last cover-sheet marker (a PDF page break —
488-    the cover sheet IS a separate page, which is the one structural thing that is reliably true of it),
489-    and failing that at the end of the marker block. If no cover-sheet marker is present, the cover sheet
490-    is empty and the article's front matter is simply the header — which is the common case and must
491-    stay cheap.

content.content:
400:_WP_MARK = re.compile(
401-    r'(nber working paper|national bureau of economic research|working paper (no\.?|series|\d)'
402-    r'|iza (discussion paper|dp)|discussion paper (no\.?|series)|cepr discussion paper'
403-    r'|preliminary(?: and incomplete)?[.:—-]? *(?:please )?do not (cite|quote)'
404-    r'|comments? welcome|this draft)', re.I)
405-_PREPRINT_MARK = re.compile(r'(arxiv[:\s]*\d{4}\.\d{4,5}|ssrn[- ]id|preprint (submitted|version)|biorxiv)', re.I)
406:_AM_MARK = re.compile(
407-    r"(accepted manuscript|author'?s? (accepted|final) (version|manuscript)|postprint"
408-    r'|published version\)? *\(refereed\)|this is the (author|accepted))', re.I)
409-# Typeset journal furniture: a running head with volume/issue/pages, or a publisher rights block.
410:_JOURNAL_MARK = re.compile(
411-    r'(volume \d+[,—-] *number \d+|vol\.? *\d+[,(]? *(no\.?|issue|\()? *\d+'
412-    r'|doi[:\s]*10\.\d{4,9}/|©\s*(the author|\d{4})|article reuse guidelines'
413-    r'|received .{0,40}(revised|accepted).{0,40}\d{4})', re.I)
414-
415-# =================================================================================================
416-# THE REGISTRY — the ONE table that says what each artifact kind IS and when it is COMPLETE.
=== registry keys ===
#: -> VERSION_PREPRINT -> derive_eligibility -> DISCOVERY_LEAD).
#:
#: WHY THE LIST GREW: the census caught the reducer scoring a GLO Discussion Paper and an arXiv preprint
#: AS JOURNAL FULLTEXT — the P0 crawling back through a gap. The old pattern knew nber|iza|arxiv but NOT
#: the working-paper vocabulary below, so an EconStor working paper (Gray 2013) passed as the journal
#: article. Whenever a new repository stamps its cover page, ADD A ROW HERE — do not touch a reducer.
PREPRINT_STAMP_REGISTRY: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
    #  registry key           header pattern (case-insensitive; matched as a fragment)
    ('nber',              r'nber working paper|national bureau of economic research'),
    ('iza',               r'iza\s+(?:dp|discussion paper)'),
    ('glo',               r'glo discussion paper'),
    ('cepr',              r'cepr discussion paper'),
    ('ssrn',              r'\bssrn\b'),
    ('repec',             r'\brepec\b'),
    ('mpra',              r'\bmpra\b'),
    ('munich_repec',      r'munich personal repec'),
    ('econstor',          r'\beconstor\b'),
    ('documento_trabajo', r'documento de trabajo'),
    ('cahier_recherche',  r'cahier de recherche'),
    ('discussion_paper',  r'discussion paper no'),
    ('working_paper',     r'working paper'),
    ('arxiv',             r'arxiv:\s*\d'),
    ('preprint',          r'\bpreprint\b'),
    ('this_version',      r'this\s+(?:draft|version):'),
    ('preliminary',       r'preliminary(?: and incomplete)?|do not (?:cite|quote) without'),
]

#: The registry compiled to ONE alternation. Named groups let a self-test report WHICH stamp fired
#: (see preprint_stamp_key). observe_text scans the HEADER ONLY: every economics paper CITES NBER
#: working papers in its REFERENCES, and a whole-text scan reads the bibliography, not the paper —
#: which is exactly how a naive scan once called the actual JEP article a working paper.
_PREPRINT_STAMP = re.compile(
    '|'.join(f'(?P<{key}>{pat})' for key, pat in PREPRINT_STAMP_REGISTRY), re.I)

content.text: Now the column vocabulary — the new data columns that make these routes executable without code.

input.old_string:
#   endpoints            real URL templates for the live probes (may be empty where there is no clean API;
#                        `discovery_via` then names the resolver route that finds this route's works).

routes:
input.new_string:
#   endpoints            real URL templates for the live probes (may be empty where there is no clean API;
#                        `discovery_via` then names the resolver route that finds this route's works).
#
#   ---- the three columns that make a route EXECUTABLE as a FULL-TEXT lane (Sol V9 §2) -------------
#   identifier_transforms  how one identifier becomes another (PMCID -> numeric -> OAI identifier). The
#                        transforms are a tiny DECLARED vocabulary (strip_prefix / format), evaluated by
#                        routes_bio._transform. A NEW REPOSITORY IS A ROW, NOT A FUNCTION.
#   selectors            where the answer lives in the response — dotted paths into JSON, or the JATS/XML
#                        element names. Changing "which field holds the PDF link" is a DATA edit.
#   batch                batch sizes the endpoint documents (PMC ID converter: 200 ids per request).
#
#   Sol V9 §2, verbatim: "Data edit: PMC endpoints, identifier transforms, metadata prefixes, and rate
#   budget. Code changes only for a new protocol/parser primitive." So the ONE parser primitive these
#   three routes needed — a JATS linearizer — is code (routes_bio.jats_to_text); every endpoint, every
#   identifier transform, every response selector and every rate budget below is DATA.
#
#   WHAT AN ADAPTER MAY NOT PUT HERE: a document_type CONCLUSION. `document_types` says what expressions
#   a route CAN yield; it never says what a fetched document IS. That is derived from the bytes, by
#   provenance.derive_expression_kind, and by nothing else.
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
routes:

    rate_policy: {host: doaj.org, min_spacing_s: 0.5, max_in_flight: 1, needs_key: false, pool: public}
    coverage_note: >-
      Directory of Open Access Journals — 13M+ articles from vetted OA journals. Its entire index is
      peer-reviewed journal expressions, so it is the strongest single index for a journal-only policy.
      IT RETURNS LINKS, NOT BYTES. A live probe on 10.1186/s13643-025-03000-0 returned exactly one
      `type: fulltext` link and it was `https://doi.org/10.1186/...` — a DOI RESOLVER, i.e. a landing
      page that redirects to the publisher. So a DOAJ hit is a CANDIDATE URL and never a document, and
      DIRECTORY MEMBERSHIP IS NOT BYTE-LEVEL VERSION PROOF. A DOAJ miss means "not indexed in DOAJ" —
      it does NOT mean "not open", and it may never be reduced to one.
    endpoints:
      search:   "https://doaj.org/api/search/articles/{query}?pageSize={limit}"
      # EXACT-DOI article search. The DOI is embedded in the PATH, so it is quoted whole (safe='').
      identity: "https://doaj.org/api/search/articles/doi%3A{doi}"
    selectors:
      results:        "results"              # the array of article records
      total:          "total"                # 0 = not indexed in DOAJ. NOT "not open".
      links:          "bibjson.link"         # [] of {type, url, content_type}
      link_kind_key:  "type"                 # the key whose value names the link kind
      link_kind_want: "fulltext"             # the kind we want. NOTE: this literal token is DATA, and
      #                                      # routes_bio compares against it — it never EMITS it, because
      #                                      # `fulltext` is a RESERVED_VALUE in the ledger's conclusion
      #                                      # guard. The adapter records n_links_matched (a count) and
      #                                      # puts DOAJ's own bytes in the blob store instead.
      link_url:       "url"
      link_media:     "content_type"         # "HTML" / "PDF" — an OBSERVATION, checked against the bytes
      record_page:    "id"                   # -> https://doaj.org/article/{id}

content.text: Now Europe PMC — with the wrong URL shape corrected and the "Subscription required" trap encoded as a selector.

input.old_string:
- adapter_id: europe_pmc
    evidence_roles: [discover.index, fetch.oa_fulltext]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript, preprint]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [europepmc_query]
    identifier_types: [doi, pmid, pmcid]
    version_resolvers: [europepmc_fulltext]
    rate_policy: {host: www.ebi.ac.uk, min_spacing_s: 1.0, needs_key: false, pool: public}
    coverage_note: >-
      Life-sciences + cross-discipline index over PMC with OA full-text XML retrieval. Full text where
      the licence permits; abstract-only otherwise (which the reducer reports as ABSTRACT_ONLY, not a gap).
    endpoints:
      search:   "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/search?query={query}&format=json&pageSize={limit}"
      fulltext: "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/{source}/{id}/fullTextXML"
input.new_string:
- adapter_id: europe_pmc
    evidence_roles: [discover.index, resolve.locations, fetch.oa_fulltext]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript, preprint]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [europepmc_query]
    identifier_types: [doi, pmid, pmcid]
    version_resolvers: [europepmc_fulltext]
    # Sol V9 §2: "official documentation does not publish a stable numeric public quota. Start at one
    # request/second and one in flight, then obey 429, Retry-After, and any returned limit headers."
    rate_policy: {host: www.ebi.ac.uk, min_spacing_s: 1.0, max_in_flight: 1, needs_key: false, pool: public}
    coverage_note: >-
      Life-sciences + cross-discipline index over PMC with OA full-text XML retrieval. Full text where
      the licence permits; abstract-only otherwise (which the reducer reports as ABSTRACT_ONLY, not a gap).
      A RECORD IS NOT A DOCUMENT: three NIH author manuscripts found live (PMC13200213, PMC13071806,
      PMC12353900) all carry a PMCID and all 404 on fullTextXML — "record found, no accessible full
      text" is a DIFFERENT fact from "identifier not found", and both are different from "not open".
    endpoints:
      search:   "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/search?query={query}&format=json&pageSize={limit}"
      # EXACT-DOI search, resultType=core (the only resultType that carries the OA/fullText fields).
      identity: 'https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/search?query=DOI%3A%22{doi}%22&resultType=core&format=json&pageSize=1'
      # ** THE SHAPE FIX (Sol V9 §2: "the currently configured /{source}/{id}/fullTextXML shape is WRONG
      # ** for PMCID retrieval"). PROVEN LIVE, 2026-07-14:
      #      /PMC/PMC12754963/fullTextXML  -> HTTP 404, 0 bytes
      #      /PMC12754963/fullTextXML      -> HTTP 200, 86,022 bytes, 34 <sec>
      # The {source}/{id} shape is for the MED/PMID-keyed endpoints, not for full text. Full text is
      # keyed by the BARE PMCID.
      fulltext: "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/europepmc/webservices/rest/{pmcid}/fullTextXML"
    selectors:
      results:          "resultList.result"
      hit_count:        "hitCount"           # 0 = not in the EPMC index. NOT "no full text exists".
      pmcid:            "pmcid"
      pmid:             "pmid"
      doi:              "doi"
      is_open_access:   "isOpenAccess"       # "Y"/"N" — an OBSERVATION. The bytes decide.
      in_epmc:          "inEPMC"
      has_pdf:          "hasPDF"
      license:          "license"
      full_text_ids:    "fullTextIdList.fullTextId"
      # ---- THE PAYWALL TRAP, ENCODED AS DATA ------------------------------------------------------
      # fullTextUrlList is NOT a list of open documents. Live on 10.1186/s13643-025-03000-0 its FIRST
      # entry was {"availability": "Subscription required", "site": "DOI", url: doi.org/...}. An adapter
      # that took fullTextUrlList[0] would fetch a PUBLISHER PAYWALL and hand it to the miner. We select
      # ONLY entries whose availabilityCode is in the open set.
      full_text_urls:        "fullTextUrlList.fullTextUrl"
      url_availability_key:  "availabilityCode"
      url_availability_open: [OA, F]         # OA = Open access; F = Free (neither means "is the VoR")
      url_value:             "url"
      url_style:             "documentStyle" # html / pdf / doi
      # ---- THE NIH-AUTHOR-MANUSCRIPT TELLS -------------------------------------------------------
      # EPMC states these plainly, and they are exactly the "NIH accepted manuscript mistaken for the
      # publisher VoR" silent failure. Recorded as version_hint OBSERVATIONS on the candidate — they
      # decide NOTHING (V9 P0: a repository label is not a fact about bytes). The VERSION VERDICT is
      # derived from the fetched front matter by provenance.derive_expression_kind.
      auth_man:         "authMan"            # "Y"/"N"
      nih_auth_man:     "nihAuthMan"         # "Y"/"N"
      epmc_auth_man:    "epmcAuthMan"        # "Y"/"N"

content.text: Now PMC — the three-hop route: ID converter → OAI GetRecord → OA service.

input.old_string:
- adapter_id: pmc
    evidence_roles: [fetch.oa_fulltext]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [pmc_id]
    identifier_types: [pmcid, pmid]
    version_resolvers: []
    rate_policy: {host: www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov, min_spacing_s: 1.2, needs_key: false, pool: public}
    coverage_note: >-
      PubMed Central OA full-text article pages by PMCID. A retrieval TARGET — its landing pages are a
      known LANDING_PAGE trap, which is exactly why completeness is derived from the bytes, not the URL.
    endpoints:
      fulltext: "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/{pmcid}/"
input.new_string:
- adapter_id: pmc
    evidence_roles: [resolve.identity, resolve.locations, fetch.oa_fulltext]
    document_types: [journal_version, accepted_manuscript]
    jurisdiction_coverage: [ANY]
    query_dialects: [pmc_id]
    identifier_types: [doi, pmcid, pmid]
    version_resolvers: [pmc_oai_jats, pmc_oa_service]
    # PMC OAI allows AT MOST 3 req/s and NO CONCURRENT REQUESTS. 0.34s is the 3/s budget; the effective
    # spacing is max(this, acquisition.SPACING_S=1.1s), because routes_bio._space_for() may only ever
    # make us MORE polite than the acquirer already is — never less. Register tool+email (NCBI usage
    # guidance): `tool` and `email` are threaded into every ID-converter call.
    rate_policy: {host: pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov, min_spacing_s: 0.34, max_in_flight: 1,
                  needs_key: false, pool: public, tool: polaris}
    coverage_note: >-
      PubMed Central, as a FULL-TEXT route: DOI/PMID -> PMCID (ID converter, batched) -> JATS via the
      OAI GetRecord -> PDF/tarball links via the OA service. A PMCID DOES NOT GUARANTEE DOWNLOADABLE OA
      FULL TEXT, and this is not a theoretical caveat: PMC13200213 has a PMCID, and the OA service
      answers `idDoesNotExist` while the OAI answers `cannotDisseminateFormat` — it is an NIH author
      manuscript outside the OA subset. Neither answer is "no document exists"; both are observations
      about PMC's OA subset, and the reducer must never collapse them into an evidence gap.
    endpoints:
      # ---- HOP 1: ID CONVERSION (DOI/PMID -> PMCID), BATCHED --------------------------------------
      # The legacy /pmc/utils/idconv/v1.0/ path 302-redirects here. We name the CANONICAL host, because
      # a redirect charges the WRONG host in the politeness budget and hides the real endpoint.
      id_convert: "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/tools/idconv/api/v1/articles/?tool={tool}&email={mailto}&format=json&ids={ids}"
      # ---- HOP 2: THE DOCUMENT (JATS full text) ---------------------------------------------------
      # The configured www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/oai/oai.cgi 301s here; pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/oai/oai.cgi
      # is a 404 that returns FORTY-EIGHT KILOBYTES OF HTML with a <body> tag in it. A route that
      # believed "<body> is present" would have called that error page a full-text document. Proven
      # live 2026-07-14: this URL returns 108,635 bytes of JATS with 17 <sec> for PMC12754963.
      oai_getrecord: "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/api/oai/v1/mh/?verb=GetRecord&identifier={oai_identifier}&metadataPrefix={metadata_prefix}"
      # ---- HOP 3: THE OA SERVICE (PDF / tarball links) --------------------------------------------
      oa_service: "https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/utils/oa/oa.fcgi?id={pmcid}"
    identifier_transforms:
      # A TINY DECLARED VOCABULARY, evaluated by routes_bio._transform. Adding a repository whose OAI
      # identifier is shaped differently is A ROW HERE, not a function.
      pmcid_numeric:  "strip_prefix:PMC"                          # PMC12754963 -> 12754963
      oai_identifier: "format:oai:pubmedcentral.nih.gov:{pmcid_numeric}"
    metadata_prefix: pmc                     # `pmc` = full-text JATS. `oai_dc` is metadata only.
    batch:
      id_convert_max_ids: 200                # the ID converter documents a 200-id ceiling
    selectors:
      # -- ID converter
      id_records:        "records"
      id_record_pmcid:   "pmcid"
      id_record_pmid:    "pmid"
      id_record_doi:     "doi"
      # A PER-RECORD ERROR INSIDE AN HTTP 200. The transport RESPONDED; this record did not resolve.
      # Live: {"status":"error","errmsg":"Identifier not found in PMC"} for 3 of our 4 probe DOIs. That
      # is a fact about PMC'S INDEX, and the ledger must keep it distinct from a 429 and from a gap.
      id_record_error:   "status"            # "error" when the id did not resolve
      id_record_errmsg:  "errmsg"
      # -- OA service (XML)
      oa_error_code:     "error/@code"       # idDoesNotExist | idIsNotOpenAccess  -> OBSERVATIONS
      oa_links:          "records/record/link"
      oa_link_format:    "@format"           # pdf | tgz
      oa_link_href:      "@href"             # NOTE: an ftp:// URL. Recorded as a candidate; the
      #                                      # executor fetches http(s) only, so the tarball stays a
      #                                      # LEAD rather than being silently dropped.
      oa_license:        "records/record/@license"
      oa_retracted:      "records/record/@retracted"
      # -- OAI GetRecord (JATS)
      oai_error_code:    "error/@code"       # cannotDisseminateFormat | idDoesNotExist
      jats_article:      "GetRecord/record/metadata/article"

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Route:
    adapter_id: str
    evidence_roles: tuple[str, ...]
    document_types: tuple[str, ...]
    jurisdiction_coverage: tuple[str, ...]
    query_dialects: tuple[str, ...]
    identifier_types: tuple[str, ...]
    version_resolvers: tuple[str, ...]
    rate_policy: dict
    coverage_note: str
    endpoints: dict
    discovery_via: tuple[str, ...] = ()
    scope_ref: dict | None = None
    # ---- Sol V9 §2: "Endpoints, identifier transforms, metadata prefixes and rate budgets are DATA." --
    # These three columns are what turn a route from a DISCOVERY probe into an executable FULL-TEXT lane
    # without writing a per-repository function. They are read by scripts/routes_bio.py; a route that
    # does not set them is unaffected.
    selectors: dict = field(default_factory=dict)              # where the answer lives in the response
    identifier_transforms: dict = field(default_factory=dict)  # PMCID -> numeric -> OAI identifier
    batch: dict = field(default_factory=dict)                  # documented batch ceilings (PMC: 200 ids)
    metadata_prefix: str = ''                                  # OAI: `pmc` (full text) vs `oai_dc` (not)
    #: THE RESOLVER ROW (Sol V9 §2/§6): auth profile, query forms, response selectors, artifact-type
    #: map, relation whitelist, rate budget. It is DATA, and it is the whole reason `routes_repo.py`
    #: contains no endpoint, no field path and no repository's name: "Adding GovInfo, EUR-Lex,
    #: CourtListener, or another repository should normally mean adding ROWS."
    resolver: dict = field(default_factory=dict)

_AM_MARK = re.compile(
    r"(accepted manuscript|author'?s? (accepted|final) (version|manuscript)|postprint"

input.new_string:
_AM_MARK = re.compile(
    r"(accepted manuscript|author'?s? (accepted|final) (version|manuscript)|postprint"
    r'|published version\)? *\(refereed\)|this is the (author|accepted)'
    # ---- THE NIH AUTHOR MANUSCRIPT (Sol V9 §2, the PMC silent failure list, verbatim: "NIH manuscript
    # ---- is mistaken for publisher VoR") ---------------------------------------------------------
    # PMC deposits NIH-funded accepted manuscripts ALONGSIDE publisher VoRs, in the same JATS dialect,
    # under the same kind of PMCID. Nothing on the wire distinguishes them — Europe PMC's `nihAuthMan`
    # flag is a REPOSITORY LABEL, and the whole V9 P0 was the project of refusing to let a repository
    # label decide what bytes ARE. So the tell has to come from THE DOCUMENT'S OWN FRONT MATTER, and
    # there are exactly two that are reliable:
    #
    #   1. the stamp PMC prints in the running head of every page of one:
    #        "Author manuscript; available in PMC 2026 Jan 01."
    #      Note this did NOT match the old pattern: `author'?s? (accepted|final) (version|manuscript)`
    #      requires the word `accepted` or `final`, and the NIH stamp says neither. The most common
    #      accepted manuscript in biomedicine was invisible to the accepted-manuscript detector.
    #   2. the JATS front-matter id <article-id pub-id-type="manuscript">NIHMS2075474</article-id>,
    #      which routes_bio.jats_to_text linearizes into the header. A VoR does not carry one.
    #
    # Both are ANCHORED (the stamp requires "in PMC"; the id requires the NIHMS prefix + digits), so a
    # paper that merely discusses author manuscripts in its prose cannot trip them. This is a STRICTLY
    # WIDENING change to an accepted-manuscript detector: it can only move bytes OUT of journal_version,
    # never into it. Under a JOURNAL-ONLY contract that is the safe direction, and it is the true one.
    r'|author manuscript;? *available in pmc|\bnihms\s*\d{3,})', re.I)
